_readme: RFC-0063 bench baseline — the reference min-times `vyrn bench --compare`
measures regressions against. JSON has no comments, so this doc lives in
the ignored `_readme`/`placeholder` fields (the comparator reads only
THIS IS A PLACEHOLDER. `placeholder: true` (and the empty `benches`) makes
`--compare` report every bench as `new` and exit 0 — comparing real timings
against an unseeded baseline is meaningless, never a failure.
To seed the REAL baseline, take it from a CI run (never a dev machine —
comparing across hardware is noise). The `bench` workflow job uploads a
`bench-json` artifact holding each example's `vyrn bench --json` output.
Download it, concatenate the per-example `benches` arrays into the `benches`
field here, drop `placeholder`/`_readme`, and commit. Refresh the same way
whenever the CI runner hardware changes.
